subUnits.title: 2.1 Location, Position and Size of FCT
notes: • Located in the center of Nigeria
• Central location chosen to promote national unity
• Represents about 0.8% of Nigeria's total land area
Reasons for Choosing Abuja as Capital:
• Accessible from all parts of Nigeria
• Not dominated by any major ethnic group
• Low population density at the time
• Decision to move capital made in 1976
• Master plan designed by Japanese architect Kenzo Tange
diagramDescriptions: Map of Nigeria showing FCT Abuja at the center with neighboring states
Map of FCT showing six area councils and their headquarters
Diagram showing reasons for choosing Abuja as capital
Timeline of FCT development from 1976 to present
subUnits.youtubeQuery: Geography of FCT Abuja location position size Nigeria site:youtube.com

quiz.q: The Federal Capital Territory (FCT) is located:
opts: In the northern part of Nigeria
In the southern part of Nigeria
In the eastern part of Nigeria
quiz.explanation: FCT Abuja is strategically located in the center of Nigeria to promote national unity.

quiz.q: FCT Abuja is bordered by all the following states EXCEPT:
quiz.explanation: FCT is bordered by Niger (North and West), Nasarawa (East), and Kogi (South). Kaduna does not border FCT.

quiz.q: The total area of FCT Abuja is approximately:
quiz.explanation: FCT covers approximately 7,315 km², representing about 0.8% of Nigeria's total land area.

quiz.q: FCT Abuja is divided into how many area councils?
quiz.explanation: FCT has six area councils: Abaji, AMAC, Bwari, Gwagwalada, Kuje, and Kwali.

quiz.q: Abuja replaced Lagos as Nigeria's capital in:
quiz.explanation: Abuja officially became Nigeria's capital on December 12, 1991.

quiz.q: One major reason for choosing Abuja as capital was its:
quiz.explanation: Central location was chosen to promote national unity and accessibility from all parts of Nigeria.

quiz.q: The master plan for Abuja was designed by:
quiz.explanation: The master plan for Abuja was designed by renowned Japanese architect Kenzo Tange.

quiz.q: FCT was chosen as a neutral territory because:
opts: It is not dominated by any major ethnic group
quiz.explanation: FCT was chosen because it's a multi-ethnic area not dominated by any of Nigeria's major ethnic groups.

quiz.q: Which of the following is NOT an area council in FCT?
quiz.explanation: Suleja is in Niger State, not FCT. The six area councils are Abaji, AMAC, Bwari, Gwagwalada, Kuje, and Kwali.

quiz.q: The decision to move Nigeria's capital to Abuja was made in:
quiz.explanation: The decision to relocate the capital was made in 1976, though the official move happened in 1991.

subUnits.title: 2.2 Political Division and Physical Setting of FCT
notes: - Contains the seat of federal government
- Contains University of Abuja Teaching Hospital
• Elevation ranges from 300m to 900m above sea level
• Zuma Rock: Iconic monolith on Abuja-Kaduna road
• Aso Rock: Prominent rock formation near Presidential Villa
• Part of the Nigerian Basement Complex
• Used for irrigation and domestic purposes
• Two distinct seasons: wet and dry
diagramDescriptions: Map of FCT showing six area councils with headquarters marked
Relief map of FCT showing major rock formations (Zuma Rock, Aso Rock)
Drainage map showing major rivers in FCT
Cross-section showing the topography of FCT
subUnits.youtubeQuery: FCT Abuja political division physical setting relief drainage site:youtube.com

quiz.q: FCT Abuja is divided into how many area councils?
quiz.explanation: FCT has six area councils: AMAC, Abaji, Bwari, Gwagwalada, Kuje, and Kwali.

quiz.q: The headquarters of Abuja Municipal Area Council (AMAC) is:
quiz.explanation: AMAC headquarters is in the Central Business District, which contains the seat of federal government.

quiz.q: Zuma Rock is located in which part of FCT?
quiz.explanation: Zuma Rock is a prominent monolith located on the Abuja-Kaduna road in the northern part of FCT.

quiz.q: The elevation of FCT ranges from:
quiz.explanation: FCT's elevation ranges from 300m to 900m above sea level, with Zuma Rock at 725m.

quiz.q: Which river forms the northern boundary of FCT?
quiz.explanation: Gurara River forms the northern boundary of FCT and is one of the major rivers in the territory.

quiz.q: The geology of FCT is mainly composed of:
quiz.explanation: FCT is part of the Nigerian Basement Complex, composed mainly of metamorphic rocks like granite and gneiss.

quiz.q: Which area council is known for pottery?
quiz.explanation: Kwali area council is famous for traditional pottery and ceramic works.

quiz.q: The climate of FCT is classified as:
quiz.explanation: FCT has a tropical savanna climate with distinct wet (April-October) and dry (November-March) seasons.

quiz.q: Aso Rock is located near:
quiz.explanation: Aso Rock is a prominent rock formation located near the Presidential Villa in Abuja.

quiz.q: Most rivers in FCT are:
quiz.explanation: Most rivers in FCT are seasonal, flowing mainly during the wet season and drying up or reducing significantly in the dry season.
